page: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-8GB is a general-purpose virtual server hosted on the KVM hypervisor. It features two shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with a local 100 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU resources are present.

Benchmark results indicate a strong contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The server achieves top-tier results in single-core stress-ng CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core performance is average, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression falls into the weak category. Memory performance is mixed; small-block cached read bandwidth is average at 421.82 GB/sec, whereas large-block write bandwidth is weak at 21.81 GB/sec. Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the bottom 25%.

Due to the shared CPU allocation, this server exhibits resource density tradeoffs, making it less optimal for sustained multi-threaded compute or high-throughput database workloads. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for entry-level workloads that benefit from strong single-threaded bursts but do not require dedicated CPU cores. Appropriate workloads include static web serving, lightweight application hosting, basic text processing, and small-scale development environments.
description: The UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-8GB is an x86_64 virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor, configured with two shared AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs operating at 2.0 GHz. It features 8.0 GB of RAM, a bundled 100 GB local SSD, and one complimentary public IPv4 address. Benchmarks reveal top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, though multi-core performance and large-block write memory bandwidth are relatively weak. Database and Redis operations also perform in the lower percentiles. Given its shared CPU allocation, this instance offers a qualitatively cost-effective option for workloads that do not require sustained multi-threaded execution. It is best suited for static web serving, lightweight application hosting, and small-scale development tasks.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-8GB is an x86_64 KVM virtual server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and a 100 GB SSD. It offers strong single-core performance but limited multi-threaded capabilities.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-2xCPU-8GB is a KVM virtual server featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and a 100 GB SSD for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A shared-core virtual server combining strong single-threaded execution with bundled SSD storage for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Two shared AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
8.0 GB of system memory and 100 GB of bundled local SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Weak multi-threaded decompression and large-block write memory bandwidth
Suited for static web serving, lightweight applications, and development environments
